ground_truth.description: Changed loop from '0..n' to '0..=n' (inclusive range). This iterates n+1 times instead of n, creating n+2 vertices instead of the documented n+1. On the last iteration (i=n), params[i] will panic with index-out-of-bounds since params has length n (indices 0..n-1). Even if it didn't panic, the simplex would have the wrong dimensionality for Nelder-Mead.
With the inclusive range 0..=n, when i=n the expression 'params[i]' accesses index n in a slice of length n, causing a panic. The original 0..n range correctly limited i to 0..n-1.
validate_simplex() is defined but never called. If it were used to validate the output of build_simplex, it would catch the off-by-one bug at runtime.
